req.title: Vital signs validation (boundary)
req.statement: An Observation with category vital-signs shall have value in clinically plausible range; out-of-range shall be flagged for review. Boundary cases on timestamps and numeric elements must be tested.
expected_outcomes: flag raised if out of range

doc.reasoning: Vital signs validation requires testing: (1) EP positive — a clinically normal heart-rate value stored without a flag; (2) NEG negative — a physiologically impossible value triggers a review task; (3) BVA boundary — values exactly at the lower and upper accepted limits are accepted/flagged correctly; (4) DT — timestamp edge cases (future timestamp, epoch-zero) expose decision-branch gaps; (5) STC — state transition from 'preliminary' to 'final' status after review-task resolution. Each test is self-contained via a clinician-auth fixture and a unique effectiveDateTime to avoid collision.

tests.name: test_vital_signs_normal_heart_rate_stored_without_flag
actions: Authenticate as clinician using <auth_fixture>
POST /Observation with category=vital-signs, code=8867-4 (heart-rate), valueQuantity=72 bpm, effectiveDateTime=now()
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: No review task reference present in response
trace: R-HLT-044: observation stored for in-range value
R-HLT-044: no flag raised for clinically plausible value
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests
from datetime import datetime, timezone

def test_vital_signs_normal_heart_rate_stored_without_flag(<auth_fixture>, base_url):
    token = <auth_fixture>.clinician_token()
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}", "Content-Type": "application/fhir+json"}
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "Observation",
        "status": "final",
        "category": [{"coding": [{"system": "http://terminology.hl7.org/CodeSystem/observation-category", "code": "vital-signs"}]}],
        "code": {"coding": [{"system": "http://loinc.org", "code": "8867-4", "display": "Heart rate"}]},
        "effectiveDateTime": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),
        "valueQuantity": {"value": 72, "unit": "beats/min", "system": "http://unitsofmeasure.org", "code": "/min"}
    }
    resp = requests.post(f"{base_url}/Observation", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 201
    body = resp.json()
    assert "id" in body
    assert body.get("status") == "final"
    assert "reviewTask" not in body

tests.name: test_vital_signs_impossible_heart_rate_raises_review_flag
preconditions: server supports review-task creation on out-of-range values
actions: Authenticate as clinician using <auth_fixture>
POST /Observation with category=vital-signs, code=8867-4, valueQuantity=350 bpm (physiologically impossible)
Capture HTTP response status, body, and any created review task reference
expected: HTTP 201 Created (observation accepted for review, not rejected)
Response body contains a reviewTask or extension flagging out-of-range
Response body status equals 'preliminary' indicating pending review
trace: R-HLT-044: out-of-range value shall be flagged for review
R-HLT-044: error pathway — review task created
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests
from datetime import datetime, timezone

def test_vital_signs_impossible_heart_rate_raises_review_flag(<auth_fixture>, base_url):
    token = <auth_fixture>.clinician_token()
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}", "Content-Type": "application/fhir+json"}
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "Observation",
        "status": "preliminary",
        "category": [{"coding": [{"system": "http://terminology.hl7.org/CodeSystem/observation-category", "code": "vital-signs"}]}],
        "code": {"coding": [{"system": "http://loinc.org", "code": "8867-4", "display": "Heart rate"}]},
        "effectiveDateTime": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),
        "valueQuantity": {"value": 350, "unit": "beats/min", "system": "http://unitsofmeasure.org", "code": "/min"}
    }
    resp = requests.post(f"{base_url}/Observation", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 201
    body = resp.json()
    flag_present = ("reviewTask" in body) or any(
        ext.get("url", "").endswith("out-of-range") for ext in body.get("extension", [])
    )
    assert flag_present, "Expected out-of-range flag or reviewTask reference"
    assert body.get("status") == "preliminary"

tests.name: test_vital_signs_boundary_heart_rate_lower_limit_accepted
preconditions: clinical lower boundary for heart-rate defined as 30 bpm
actions: Authenticate as clinician using <auth_fixture>
POST /Observation with valueQuantity=30 bpm (exact lower boundary)
Capture response and check for flag
POST /Observation with valueQuantity=29 bpm (one below lower boundary)
Capture response and check for flag
trace: R-HLT-044: boundary cases on numeric elements must be tested
R-HLT-044: out-of-range shall be flagged for review
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests
from datetime import datetime, timezone

def <post_observation>(base_url, token, value):
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}", "Content-Type": "application/fhir+json"}
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "Observation",
        "status": "final",
        "category": [{"coding": [{"system": "http://terminology.hl7.org/CodeSystem/observation-category", "code": "vital-signs"}]}],
        "code": {"coding": [{"system": "http://loinc.org", "code": "8867-4"}]},
        "effectiveDateTime": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),
        "valueQuantity": {"value": value, "unit": "beats/min", "system": "http://unitsofmeasure.org", "code": "/min"}
    }
    return requests.post(f"{base_url}/Observation", json=payload, headers=headers)

def test_vital_signs_boundary_heart_rate_lower_limit_accepted(<auth_fixture>, base_url):
    token = <auth_fixture>.clinician_token()
    resp_at = <post_observation>(base_url, token, 30)
    assert resp_at.status_code == 201
    body_at = resp_at.json()
    assert body_at.get("status") == "final"
    assert "reviewTask" not in body_at
    resp_below = <post_observation>(base_url, token, 29)
    assert resp_below.status_code == 201
    body_below = resp_below.json()
    flag = ("reviewTask" in body_below) or any(
        e.get("url", "").endswith("out-of-range") for e in body_below.get("extension", [])
    )
    assert flag, "Value below lower boundary must be flagged"

tests.name: test_vital_signs_boundary_heart_rate_upper_limit_flagged
preconditions: clinical upper boundary for heart-rate defined as 250 bpm
actions: Authenticate as clinician using <auth_fixture>
POST /Observation with valueQuantity=250 bpm (exact upper boundary — accepted)
POST /Observation with valueQuantity=251 bpm (one above upper boundary — flagged)
trace: R-HLT-044: boundary cases on numeric elements must be tested
R-HLT-044: out-of-range shall be flagged for review

tests.name: test_vital_signs_future_timestamp_rejected_or_flagged
preconditions: server enforces effectiveDateTime must not be in the future
actions: Authenticate as clinician using <auth_fixture>
POST /Observation with a valid vital-signs value but effectiveDateTime set 1 year in the future
Capture HTTP status and OperationOutcome
expected: OperationOutcome.issue contains a diagnostic referencing invalid timestamp
trace: R-HLT-044: boundary cases on timestamps must be tested
R-HLT-044: invalid timestamp is an error pathway
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests
from datetime import datetime, timezone, timedelta

def test_vital_signs_future_timestamp_rejected_or_flagged(<auth_fixture>, base_url):
    token = <auth_fixture>.clinician_token()
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}", "Content-Type": "application/fhir+json"}
    future_ts = (datetime.now(timezone.utc) + timedelta(days=365)).isoformat()
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "Observation",
        "status": "final",
        "category": [{"coding": [{"system": "http://terminology.hl7.org/CodeSystem/observation-category", "code": "vital-signs"}]}],
        "code": {"coding": [{"system": "http://loinc.org", "code": "8867-4"}]},
        "effectiveDateTime": future_ts,
        "valueQuantity": {"value": 72, "unit": "beats/min", "system": "http://unitsofmeasure.org", "code": "/min"}
    }
    resp = requests.post(f"{base_url}/Observation", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert resp.status_code in (400, 422), f"Expected 400/422, got {resp.status_code}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("resourceType") == "OperationOutcome"
    issues = body.get("issue", [])
    assert any("timestamp" in str(i.get("diagnostics", "")).lower() or
               "date" in str(i.get("diagnostics", "")).lower() for i in issues)

tests.name: test_vital_signs_review_task_state_transition_on_resolution
preconditions: An out-of-range Observation exists with an associated review Task in 'requested' status
<create_flagged_observation> helper creates said Observation and returns task_id
actions: Authenticate as clinician using <auth_fixture>
Create out-of-range Observation via <create_flagged_observation>, capture task_id
PUT /Task/{task_id} with status='completed' to resolve the review
GET /Observation/{obs_id} and verify status transitions to 'final'
expected: No open review task reference remains on the Observation
trace: R-HLT-044: error pathway — review task created
R-HLT-044: expected outcome — observation stored; flag raised if out of range
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_vital_signs_review_task_state_transition_on_resolution(<auth_fixture>, base_url):
    token = <auth_fixture>.clinician_token()
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}", "Content-Type": "application/fhir+json"}
    obs_id, task_id = <create_flagged_observation>(base_url, token)
    task_update = {
        "resourceType": "Task",
        "id": task_id,
        "status": "completed",
        "intent": "order"
    }
    put_resp = requests.put(f"{base_url}/Task/{task_id}", json=task_update, headers=headers)
    assert put_resp.status_code == 200
    get_resp = requests.get(f"{base_url}/Observation/{obs_id}", headers=headers)
    assert get_resp.status_code == 200
    obs_body = get_resp.json()
    assert obs_body.get("status") == "final", "Observation should transition to final after task completion"
    open_tasks = [e for e in obs_body.get("extension", []) if "review" in e.get("url", "").lower()]
    assert not open_tasks, "No open review extensions should remain after resolution"
